verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a secreted growth factor with no transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. It is synthesized with a signal peptide and released into the extracellular space, where it binds VEGFR1/2/3 and neuropilins on endothelial cells. Heparin-binding isoforms (VEGF165, VEGF189, VEGF206) deposit on heparan sulfate proteoglycans in ECM, but this is reversible matrix sequestration, not wash-resistant covalent anchoring to a TM partner. Clinical anti-VEGFA agents (bevacizumab, ranibizumab, aflibercept) target the soluble circulating/extracellular pool, not a cell-surface protein body. No cell-state-induced surface display, no lysosomal exocytosis, no tissue-restricted surface form, no TM precursor isoform, no stable covalent surface attachment documented.
